dsl_rule.translation_notes: Used vol_ratio_20 as the available volume ratio indicator. Signal specifies NYSE upside/downside ratio with 7:1 threshold and 1 consecutive day requirement. Price above previous close confirms directional bias. Exit on ratio falling below 5:1 or 2% stop loss.

backtest_rule.reason: Used vol_ratio_20 as the available volume ratio indicator. Signal specifies NYSE upside/downside ratio with 7:1 threshold and 1 consecutive day requirement. Price above previous close confirms directional bias. Exit on ratio falling below 5:1 or 2% stop loss.

source_rule_text: A trader should enter long positions when the daily ratio of upside to downside volume on the NYSE exceeds a defined threshold on a specified number of consecutive occurrences, using volume ratio extremes as a confirmation signal for directional bias.
